title: Delhi 5-Day City Break
metaTitle: Delhi 5-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 5-day trip to Delhi, India: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Red Fort and Qutub Minar Complex. Prices from $1379 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 5 days discovering Delhi, India — Extreme continental climate: scorching, dry summers (highs 38-40°C in May-June), a humid monsoon July-September with intense but short downpours, and cool, foggy winters (highs 18-21°C, night lows near 5°C, with dense fog delaying flights in late December-January). October to March, when daytime highs are 20-29°C and skies are clear; avoid the pre-monsoon heat of May-June (highs above 38°C) unless staying mostly indoors/AC. This itinerary blends Red Fort, Qutub Minar Complex, India Gate with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
4 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Red Fort, Qutub Minar Complex, India Gate
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Delhi
itinerary.description: Land at Indira Gandhi International Airport (DEL) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Red Fort — Mughal-era fortified palace and UNESCO World Heritage Site. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Delhi: Qutub Minar Complex & Humayun's Tomb
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Qutub Minar Complex — Soaring 12th-century minaret and Indo-Islamic ruins. In the afternoon, continue to Humayun's Tomb — Precursor to the Taj Mahal, set in Mughal charbagh gardens. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Delhi: National Museum & Akshardham Temple exhibition & boat ride
itinerary.description: Morning visit to National Museum — Sweeping collection of Indian art, sculpture and artifacts. In the afternoon, continue to Akshardham Temple exhibition & boat ride — Modern temple complex with multimedia shows (temple entry itself is free). Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Agra (Taj Mahal)
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Agra (Taj Mahal), a popular side trip from Delhi. Return to Delhi in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Purana Qila or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to DEL for your departure flight.

travelTips: Take an early-morning Gatimaan or Vande Bharat Express to Agra for a same-day Taj Mahal trip rather than a slow road journey.
Book Red Fort and Humayun's Tomb tickets online via the ASI website to skip ticket-counter queues.
Bargain firmly (start around 40-50% of the quoted price) in Chandni Chowk and Sarojini Nagar.
Avoid travel agents who approach you unsolicited near Connaught Place or Paharganj claiming "government tourism office" status.
Winter fog (late Dec-Jan) regularly delays or diverts flights — build buffer time into connecting itineraries.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Light breathable cotton clothing for summer heat
A warm jacket and layers for foggy December-January mornings
A scarf/dupatta for covering shoulders and head at religious sites
Comfortable, sturdy walking shoes for uneven Old Delhi lanes
thingsToDo: Old Delhi cycle-rickshaw food tour at dawn
thingsToAvoid: Unregistered "government emporium" touts near Connaught Place and tourist sites pushing overpriced tours.
Unmetered taxis/autos at the airport — use the prepaid counter or a ride-hailing app instead.
Drinking tap water — stick to sealed bottled or filtered water.
Walking alone in poorly lit backstreets of Paharganj or Old Delhi late at night.
Touts at New Delhi Railway Station claiming your train is "cancelled" and redirecting you to a private travel office.
topCafes: Greater Kailash M Block Market cafes
nightlife: Thursday evening qawwali at Nizamuddin Dargah
adventureActivities: Old Delhi cycle-rickshaw food tour at dawn
Hot-air ballooning day trips near NCR
Trekking day trip to the Aravalli hills (Damdama Lake)
luxuryExperiences: Suite stay at The Imperial or The Leela Palace
Private heritage walk with a Mughal-era historian
Fine dining tasting menu at Indian Accent
Spa retreat at The Oberoi New Delhi
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset visit to Humayun's Tomb gardens
Rooftop dinner with a Qutub Minar view
budgetExperiences: Street food crawl in Chandni Chowk
Free community meal (langar) at Gurudwara Bangla Sahib
localEtiquette: Remove shoes before entering temples, gurudwaras and mosques; cover your head with a scarf at Sikh gurudwaras (cloth is provided if needed).
Use your right hand for eating, giving/receiving items and handshakes — the left hand is considered unclean.
Dress modestly at religious sites and in Old Delhi — shoulders and knees covered is appreciated, especially for women.
Bargaining is expected in bazaars like Chandni Chowk and Sarojini Nagar, but is not appropriate in malls or fixed-price shops.
Public displays of affection are frowned upon at religious and heritage sites.
localTransportGuide: The Delhi Metro is fast, air-conditioned and covers most tourist areas — buy a rechargeable smart card to skip queues. Auto-rickshaws (insist on the meter or agree a fare beforehand) and Ola/Uber are widely available; at the airport, use the prepaid taxi counter or Metro Airport Express Line rather than touts.
bestTimeToVisit: October to March, when daytime highs are 20-29°C and skies are clear; avoid the pre-monsoon heat of May-June (highs above 38°C) unless staying mostly indoors/AC.
weather: Extreme continental climate: scorching, dry summers (highs 38-40°C in May-June), a humid monsoon July-September with intense but short downpours, and cool, foggy winters (highs 18-21°C, night lows near 5°C, with dense fog delaying flights in late December-January).
visaInformation: Most foreign nationals need an e-Visa (apply online 4-120 days before travel; tourist e-Visa allows stays up to 90 days for many nationalities). Indian passport holders need no visa. Overseas Citizen of India (OCI) cardholders and PIO cardholders travel visa-free with lifetime multiple entry, provided their OCI card is registered/linked to a valid foreign passport — a common arrangement for NRI travelers visiting family.
averageFlightTime: ~9.5h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Delhi?
faqs.answer: Most foreign nationals need an e-Visa (apply online 4-120 days before travel; tourist e-Visa allows stays up to 90 days for many nationalities). Indian passport holders need no visa. Overseas Citizen of India (OCI) cardholders and PIO cardholders travel visa-free with lifetime multiple entry, provided their OCI card is registered/linked to a valid foreign passport — a common arrangement for NRI travelers visiting family.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Delhi?
faqs.answer: October to March, when daytime highs are 20-29°C and skies are clear; avoid the pre-monsoon heat of May-June (highs above 38°C) unless staying mostly indoors/AC. Extreme continental climate: scorching, dry summers (highs 38-40°C in May-June), a humid monsoon July-September with intense but short downpours, and cool, foggy winters (highs 18-21°C, night lows near 5°C, with dense fog delaying flights in late December-January).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Delhi safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Delhi is safe for tourists who stay alert, particularly around tourist hotspots and transport hubs — avoid displaying valuables and use hotel-recommended transport after dark.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Delhi?
faqs.answer: The local currency is INR. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Lucas Kowalski
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Connaught Place and Gurudwara Bangla Sahib. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Chen Costa
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: Booked this as a couple trip and it was well paced. Chandni Chowk in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Fatima Ibrahim
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Safdarjung Tomb alone was worth the trip, but combined with Connaught Place this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Noah Larsen
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Our trip to Delhi was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Safdarjung Tomb was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Liam Smith
reviews.title: Perfect for our honeymoon
reviews.body: Our trip to Delhi was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Akshardham Temple exhibition & boat ride was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: James Ibrahim
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: Our trip to Delhi was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Connaught Place was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Marco Tanaka
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Safdarjung Tomb and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Sarah van Dijk
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: Delhi is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Safdarjung Tomb. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.
